6badea1f-5c4e-431c-a60f-a233102411cb.title: DNS_CONFULENCE
6badea1f-5c4e-431c-a60f-a233102411cb.summary:
DNS_CONFULENCE SUMMARY

WHAT THIS DOCUMENT COVERS
This runbook provides comprehensive troubleshooting procedures for DNS resolution issues and Netskope routing problems affecting application and service accessibility. It addresses scenarios where users cannot reach internal or external resources due to DNS failures, misrouted traffic, or Netskope steering policy issues. The document is designed for network engineers and IT support personnel handling connectivity escalations.

KEY TECHNICAL CONCEPTS

Netskope DNS steering intercepts DNS queries for domains in its policy and resolves them to gateway IPs in the 191.x.x.x range, routing traffic through Netskope for security inspection. If a Netskope-steered domain resolves to any IP outside 191.x.x.x (like 103, 52, 142, 104, 34, 13, 8, or 40 ranges), traffic bypasses Netskope and gets blocked by corporate security policy, making applications appear unreachable.

DNS resolution issues manifest as NXDOMAIN errors, ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED, connection timeouts, or wrong IP addresses being returned. Internal hostname failures typically indicate missing zone records, BIND9 service problems, or zone file syntax errors.

Path determination depends on the resolved IP range and Netskope status: 191.x.x.x indicates correct Netskope routing with application-layer issues; non-191 IPs with Netskope active indicates routing bypass; non-191 IPs without Netskope indicates general DNS problems.

DNS cache issues cause stale IP addresses to persist after record updates, requiring cache flushes at both client and server levels. BIND9 zone management requires serial number incrementation in SOA records and zone reloads using rndc commands.

DIAGNOSTIC COMMANDS

Host command for Mac/Linux: host <application-url>
Nslookup for Windows: nslookup <application-url>
Direct DNS server query: nslookup <hostname> <dns-server-ip>
Public DNS test: nslookup <domain> 8.8.8.8
BIND9 status check: sudo systemctl status bind9
Zone status verification: sudo rndc zonestatus <zone-name>
Zone file syntax validation: sudo named-checkzone <zone-name> /etc/bind/zones/<zone-file>
Mac DNS flush: sudo dscacheutil -flushcache and sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder
Windows DNS flush: ipconfig /flushdns
Linux DNS flush: sudo systemd-resolve --flush-caches
Server cache flush: sudo rndc flush

RESOLUTION APPROACHES

Netskope routing fixes: disconnect and reconnect Netskope client, wait 10 seconds between disconnect/reconnect, flush client DNS cache if reconnection fails, escalate for steering policy updates if domain not in policy
DNS cache resolution: flush client-side cache, flush server-side cache using rndc flush, re-test resolution after each flush
Zone file repairs: add missing A records, increment SOA serial numbers, validate with named-checkzone, reload zones with sudo rndc reload <zone-name>
Application-layer troubleshooting: verify other applications work, check for login versus connection errors, confirm VPN requirements, escalate to application teams if service is down

WHEN TO USE THIS DOCUMENT
Use this runbook when users report inaccessible applications, websites, portals, internal servers, or any connection errors including ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED, timeouts, NXDOMAIN errors, or resolution failures after DNS configuration changes.
